summary_oneline: Add generic Jira status-transition detection and configurable action dispatch to JiraSpecialist

localization.role: Event classifier — currently hardcodes only closed/ready_for_test transitions; all others collapse to 'updated'
Event router — only routes jira.created, jira.assigned, jira.ready_for_test; ignores everything else
Webhook POST handler — builds event payload but does not include from_status/to_status fields
Config model — may need transition_actions field
Reference pattern — shows how GitHub events are filtered and dispatched to handler methods
Hook base class — no changes needed, interface is sufficient

constraints.constraint: Existing 3 hardcoded handlers (created, assigned, ready_for_test) must continue working unchanged
JiraWebhookHook._classify_event is a static method shared by all consumers — changes affect all JiraSpecialist instances
Payload enrichment must be backward-compatible — existing payload keys must remain
Follow GitHubReviewer pattern for consistency (webhook setup, hook event handling, action dispatch)

whats_new: jira.transitioned event type emitted for all status changes
TransitionAction registry on JiraSpecialist for configurable transition→handler mappings
from_status and to_status fields in webhook payload for all status-change events
Built-in transition handlers: notify_channel, trigger_agent, log_transition
whats_changed: _classify_event: emit 'transitioned' for status changes instead of falling through to 'updated'
_handle_post: enrich payload with from_status/to_status extracted from changelog
handle_hook_event: add jira.transitioned routing to transition dispatch
non_goals: Changing how jira.created, jira.assigned, jira.ready_for_test work today
Jira-side webhook subscription management (manual setup like today)
Two-way sync (writing transitions back to Jira from the agent)

recommended_next_command.rationale: All code locations are identified with high confidence, the pattern (GitHubReviewer) is well-established, and no architectural fork exists.
